query: Are their any blue colored Guyalna woldai in Spain written by Christer Hansson?

treatments.articleTitle: The cicadas (Hemiptera: Cicadidae) of Panama including the description of six new species, three new combinations, one new synonymy, and nine new records
treatments.speciesDesc: The new species is a type of beetle, specifically a member of the family Cerambycidae, commonly known as longhorn beetles. It is described as having a distinctive body length and coloration that sets it apart from other Panamanian species of Guyalna. The species can be distinguished by its unique characteristics, such as its greenish tawny head and thorax with an ochraceous abdomen, and its infuscated fore wings.

Scale bar: A, 2 cm; B, 5 mm; C–E, 2 mm; F–G, 1 mm; H–I, 2 mm.

FIGURE 3. Comparison of the dorsum and timbal covers of Proarna championi n. comb. to the type species of the genera Proarna and Pacarina. A, Proarna championi n. comb. dorsum; B, Proarna hilaris dorsum; C, Pacarina puella dorsum; D, Proarna championi n. comb. timbal cover; E, Proarna hilaris timbal cover; F, Pacarina puella timbal cover. Scale bar: A–C, 5 mm; D–F, 2 mm.
